No Poverty.reason: The course's focus on life insurance and annuities directly contributes to social protection systems (Target 1.3) and builds financial resilience against economic shocks for the vulnerable (Target 1.5).

Zero Hunger.reason: The course content on actuarial mathematics for life contingencies has no discernible connection to food security, nutrition, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course's core topics, 'Survival Distributions' and 'Life Tables,' are fundamental to life insurance, which provides financial risk protection, directly supporting SDG 3.8.
evidence: Life Tables and Select Life Tables

Quality Education.reason: The course provides 'advanced professional knowledge in risk management and insurance,' directly equipping students with relevant technical skills for employment and decent jobs, aligning with Target 4.4.
evidence: 習得風險管理與保險之相關進階專業知識 (Students will acquire advanced professional knowledge in risk management and insurance field.)

Gender Equality.reason: While the course mentions ethical considerations, there is no specific evidence linking the content or learning outcomes to gender equality targets.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: The course content on actuarial mathematics for life contingencies has no discernible connection to clean water, sanitation, or water resource management.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course content on actuarial mathematics for life contingencies has no discernible connection to affordable, reliable, or clean energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course directly strengthens the capacity of domestic financial institutions in the insurance sector through actuarial training, explicitly supporting Target 8.10.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course teaches advanced mathematical and modeling techniques, such as 'discrete-time Markov Chain models,' fostering innovation and upgrading technological capabilities within the financial services industry (Target 9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course's focus on life insurance and actuarial principles supports the development of social protection policies (Target 10.4) and sound financial institution regulation (Target 10.5), contributing to reduced inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course's focus on life insurance offers a very tenuous link to Target 11.5 by providing a mechanism for individual economic loss reduction, though not directly related to disaster resilience for communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course's core competency in 'social responsibility and business ethics' offers a weak, inferred link to encouraging sustainable corporate practices (Target 12.6), though not explicitly focused on consumption or production.
evidence: 理解與辨別社會責任與企業倫理議題 (Students will understand and be able to identify the ethical considerations of business situations.)

Climate Action.reason: The course content on actuarial mathematics for life contingencies has no discernible connection to climate change mitigation, adaptation, or resilience.

Life Below Water.reason: The course content on actuarial mathematics for life contingencies has no discernible connection to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: The course content on actuarial mathematics for life contingencies has no discernible connection to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Actuarial science, through calculating liabilities and reserves, is fundamental to the solvency and accountability of insurance companies, thereby contributing to effective and transparent financial institutions (Target 16.6).
evidence: 社會責任與企業倫理議題 (social responsibility and business ethics)

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course builds statistical capacity through demographic and financial modeling (Target 17.19) and contributes to macroeconomic stability via a robust insurance sector (Target 17.13).
